quick_facts.label: What it is
quick_facts.value: An MPP is a postgraduate, practice-oriented public-policy degree. Program design differs by university: Harvard describes a social-science-based toolkit for action, while Oxford describes an intensive taught degree for current and future public-service leaders.

quick_facts.label: Typical study length
quick_facts.value: Duration is institution-specific rather than uniform: Oxford lists its full-time MPP as 12 months, while Harvard Kennedy School lists its MPP as two years.

quick_facts.label: Academic background
quick_facts.value: A strong bachelor's degree is a common baseline. For example, Harvard requires a bachelor's degree and evidence of quantitative proficiency; Oxford accepts applicants from any discipline but expects a first-class or strong upper-second-class equivalent academic record.

quick_facts.label: Work experience
quick_facts.value: Professional experience can strengthen an application but is not universally compulsory. Harvard says it is not strictly required and notes that incoming students typically have 2–3 years of experience; Oxford says applicants are not required to have prior experience, although many successful candidates bring at least one or two years.

quick_facts.label: GRE relevance
quick_facts.value: GRE requirements are program-specific. Harvard's published MPP requirements state that the GRE or GMAT is required, with a possible waiver for applicants who have completed specified quantitative coursework; ETS states GRE scores are reportable for five years.

quick_facts.label: Cost context
quick_facts.value: Fees and living costs vary sharply by country and school and change by intake. For 2026–27, Oxford lists an annual MPP course fee of £54,450 for overseas students, excluding living and possible placement-related costs; Harvard lists $72,106 in MPP tuition and mandatory fees before indirect living costs.

sections.heading: Eligibility and academic fit
paragraphs: Applicants from India ordinarily apply after completing a recognised bachelor's degree, but the exact equivalency, grade threshold, quantitative preparation and English-language evidence are set by each university. Harvard's MPP requires a strong bachelor's record plus quantitative proficiency demonstrated through undergraduate economics, statistics or calculus; Oxford's MPP accepts applicants from any discipline and specifies a first-class or strong upper-second-class equivalent as its minimum academic benchmark for the 2026–27 entry cycle.

Professional experience is valuable for many policy degrees because programmes emphasise applied problem-solving and leadership. It should not, however, be assumed to be mandatory: Harvard calls work experience an admissions advantage rather than a strict requirement, and Oxford says prior experience is not required even though many successful candidates have it.

English testing must be checked course by course. Oxford's 2026–27 MPP page, for example, specifies its higher English-language level and gives test-specific minimums; applicants should rely on the target university's current admissions page rather than applying a generic IELTS or TOEFL threshold.
bullets: Build evidence of quantitative readiness where required: transcripts and relevant coursework are important even for applicants whose undergraduate major was not economics or mathematics.
Treat GRE/GMAT policy as university- and intake-specific. A GRE parent node is useful for planning, but it does not establish that every overseas MPP requires the GRE.
Prepare official transcripts and, where applicable, certified translations or the university's stated credential documentation.

sections.heading: Duration and study format
paragraphs: There is no single standard duration for an overseas MPP. Oxford presents a full-time, 12-month MPP; Harvard Kennedy School presents a two-year MPP. This difference affects total tuition exposure, opportunity cost, visa planning and the sequencing of internships or capstones.

Most campus-based MPPs combine intensive taught work with applied assignments. Oxford states that its course is full-time and requires attendance in Oxford, while Harvard's published degree requirements include full-time, in-residence enrolment.
bullets: Compare the programme's stated length, residency requirement and full-time credit load before applying.
For one-year programmes, confirm whether a summer policy placement, project or report is mandatory and budget time and funds accordingly.
For two-year programmes, check the timing of internships, capstone work and any joint-degree options.

sections.heading: What students study
paragraphs: MPP curricula typically combine policy analysis with the institutional and practical dimensions of implementation. Harvard's first-year core develops competence in policy analysis, empirical methods, negotiation, economics and politics/ethics, and its second-year work includes a policy-area concentration and Policy Analysis Exercise capstone.

Oxford's current published core includes economics for public policy, politics of policymaking, law and public policy, evidence in public policy and policy challenges. It also requires a policy-report placement, intended to apply course learning in a public-policy context.

Electives allow specialisation, but offerings change. Oxford notes that its option and applied-policy modules vary by year; examples from prior years include climate change, cybersecurity, education policy, migration, public budgeting, communications and negotiations.
bullets: Look for analytical training: economics, statistics or empirical methods, evidence use and policy evaluation.
Look for implementation training: politics, law, public management, negotiation, communication and stakeholder decision-making.
Compare the final applied requirement: policy memo, client project, practicum, placement, capstone or thesis. An MPP need not be a research-master's degree.

sections.heading: Application components and planning
paragraphs: Application packages are holistic and should connect prior academic or professional work to a credible public-policy goal. Harvard lists an online application, essays, résumé/CV, three recommendations, transcripts, standardised-test scores where required or not waived, English-language results where applicable, and an application fee or waiver. Oxford's MPP process requires a personal statement, CV, transcripts, three references and two written essays, among other course-specific requirements.

Deadlines are intake-specific and can close many months before the programme begins. For example, Oxford's page for 2026–27 reports that applications are closed and identifies a December-cycle application process for that intake; Harvard's programme page lists December 2026 as its displayed MPP admission deadline. These dates are dated examples, not deadlines to reuse for a later cycle.

Applicants should request references and official academic documents early, complete language testing in time for conditional-offer requirements, and verify whether the target school expects scores to be sent directly by ETS or another testing body.
bullets: Start with a shortlist whose curriculum, work-experience profile, location and budget fit your policy interests.
Use the statement of purpose and policy essays to demonstrate problem definition, evidence-based reasoning, leadership or public-service commitment, and realistic career intent.
Check each course's current application portal for document formats, word limits, referee submission rules, fee-waiver rules and test-reporting codes.
Do not use a previous cycle's deadline, fee or test rule without reconfirming it on the official course page.

sections.heading: Costs, funding and financial planning
paragraphs: The financial commitment includes more than tuition: applicants should budget for university fees, accommodation and food, health insurance where applicable, travel, visa and immigration-health charges where applicable, books/materials, application and test fees, and any practicum or fieldwork costs. Oxford explicitly states that its course fee excludes accommodation and other living costs, and notes that a policy-report placement can create additional expenses.

As dated 2026–27 examples, Oxford lists an overseas annual MPP fee of £54,450, a £8,200 deposit, and estimated Oxford living costs of £1,405–£2,105 per month for a single full-time student. Harvard lists 2026–27 MPP tuition and mandatory fees of $72,106, and separately notes that its full cost of attendance includes indirect costs such as living expenses. These figures are not estimates for all overseas MPPs and may change before a future intake.

Funding is competitive and varies by citizenship, university, college, external sponsor and admission cycle. Oxford and Harvard both direct admitted or prospective students toward programme/university funding opportunities; applicants should examine whether awards cover tuition only or also living costs, insurance and travel.
bullets: Create a currency-aware budget for the full programme length, not only the first term's tuition.
Separate guaranteed funding from scholarships still under consideration before making an enrolment decision.
Review deposit deadlines and refund conditions before accepting an offer.
Check official student-visa financial-evidence requirements for the destination country separately from the university's admission financial declaration.

sections.heading: Possible next options after the degree
paragraphs: An MPP is commonly positioned for applied policy work rather than automatically as research preparation. Oxford explicitly describes its MPP as a professional degree focused on policy-making and analysis and says it is not designed to prepare students for research-based study such as its DPhil in Public Policy.

Next steps can therefore include policy, government, multilateral, nonprofit, consulting or private-sector roles that use policy-analysis and implementation skills, subject to an individual's prior experience, work authorisation and employer requirements. Oxford reports alumni in public-sector roles, multilaterals and the third sector, while Harvard publishes an employment-sector snapshot spanning public/international-governmental, nonprofit and private sectors.

Students seeking a research-focused route should assess research-methods preparation carefully. Oxford offers a Public Policy 1+1 pathway in which applicants submit separate applications for the MPP and MSc in Public Policy Research in the same cycle; Harvard also describes joint and concurrent degree options with disciplines such as business, law, medicine and design.
bullets: For professional policy careers, use electives, client projects, internships and the capstone to create a portfolio of policy writing and evidence-based recommendations.
For doctoral ambitions, verify that the intended MPP has sufficient research methods, quantitative training and dissertation or thesis opportunities—or plan a separate research master's route.
For employment abroad after graduation, investigate post-study work rules and employer sponsorship conditions in the destination country at the time of application; these rules can change.
